justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Atomic acceptance identified and solved via Redis HSETNX in Lua script — functionally equivalent to SET NX, eliminates race conditions entirely. (2) In-memory state (Redis) recommended with explicit durability trade-off acknowledged ('Redis failure loses all in-flight bids' risk + 'data not durable by default' sacrifice in trade-offs table). (3) Fallback auto-assignment on timer expiry fully designed with Timer Service polling sorted sets and selecting highest-rated courier. (4) Hot key risk addressed indirectly — throughput analysis shows 500×30=15K ops/sec is 'trivial for Redis' on single instance, and Redis Cluster mentioned for failover, though explicit hot-key-per-shard risk could be stronger. (5) Durability vs. latency trade-off explicitly covered in trade-offs table comparing Redis vs PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE.

observations.text: Lua script for atomic acceptance is superior to simple SET NX — handles eligibility check + state transition atomically in one round trip
Comprehensive fallback auto-assignment: Timer Service polls Redis sorted sets, transitions expired windows, selects highest-rated courier
Durability vs latency trade-off explicitly called out with alternatives considered (PostgreSQL too slow, DynamoDB higher latency)
Clock skew risk identified and mitigated by using Redis TIME command rather than wall clocks
Explicit 'Pitfalls to Avoid' section warns against database-first approach, optimistic locking, and keyspace notifications
Pub/Sub fire-and-forget trade-off acknowledged with reconnection/heartbeat mitigation

Hot key risk for Redis sharding not explicitly called out — analysis dismisses scale as trivial for single instance but doesn't address what happens if bid windows concentrate on one shard in a cluster setup
Implementation guidance is unusually thorough — file structure, tech stack, anti-patterns all specified

agent_output_excerpt:
# Courier Bidding System — Architecture Design

## Executive Summary

The courier bidding system is fundamentally a **real-time first-write-wins coordination problem** with a hard 15-second deadline. The architecture centers on **Redis as the atomic coordination layer** (Lua scripts for race-free acceptance), **WebSockets for real-time push**, and a **timer-based fallback for auto-assignment**.
